response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've actually been curious about this topic myself, despite not being active on social media. I remember reading an article once that mentioned how even those of us who aren't online can be affected by the spread of misinformation in our daily lives.

My sister-in-law is a teacher and she tells me all the time about dealing with fake news in her classroom. As someone with my own master's degree, I can see why critical thinking skills are so important when it comes to evaluating information found online.

I think one way people like myself handle this issue is by focusing on media literacy from an early age.

Based on her educational background and moderate openness score, she likely values intellectual engagement with the topic while being somewhat reserved in expressing opinions.

Her high agreeableness suggests she approaches the subject matter collaboratively rather than confrontationally.

As someone who is not depressed when engaging with this question (since it's about managing fake content), I can provide a thoughtful perspective on combating misinformation without personal bias.
